title: 8 Herbal Creams For Chronic Hepatitis
intro_desc: If you're living with chronic hepatitis, you might be interested in trying herbal creams as a natural way to manage your symptoms. These creams work by applying the healing properties of herbs directly to the skin, which can help to soothe and calm inflammation in the liver. One example of an herb that's often used in these creams is Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric. Curcuma longa contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce liver damage and promote healing.  Another herb that's often used in herbal creams for chronic hepatitis is Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle. This plant has been used for centuries to support liver health and protect against damage. Silybum marianum contains a compound called silymarin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce liver inflammation and promote liver regeneration.  Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, is another herb that's sometimes used in herbal creams for chronic hepatitis. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. It's also been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the liver against damage.  Using herbal creams for chronic hepatitis can bring many benefits to your life. For one thing, it can help you to manage your symptoms and feel more comfortable. It can also help to reduce the risk of complications and promote liver regeneration. Additionally, using herbal creams can be a gentle and non-invasive way to support liver health, which can be especially important if you're not a fan of taking medications or if you're looking for a more natural approach to managing your symptoms.  When you use herbal creams for chronic hepatitis, you can apply them directly to the skin to help soothe and calm inflammation in the liver. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, or digestive issues. By applying the healing properties of herbs directly to the skin, you can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the liver, which can lead to a range of benefits for your overall health and wellbeing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, a powerful bioactive constituent that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis. Turmeric's active compound also has potent antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which may help combat the underlying causes of the disease. The bioavailability of curcumin in Curcuma longa creams is enhanced by its combination with other beneficial compounds like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which work synergistically to provide therapeutic benefits. By targeting multiple pathways involved in chronic hepatitis, Curcuma longa creams may offer a promising natural treatment option for managing this condition.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) to the distilled water and heat it until it dissolves.
Combine the dissolved turmeric mixture with the coconut oil in the double boiler. Stir until well mixed.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Then, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture again in the double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until it solidifies into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols that have antioxidant properties. Silymarin helps protect liver cells from damage and reduces inflammation in the liver, which can be beneficial for people with chronic hepatitis. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce liver scarring and improve liver function. Silybum marianum creams contain a compound called silibinin, which has been shown to improve liver enzyme levels and reduce oxidative stress in the liver. By reducing inflammation and improving liver function, Silybum marianum creams may help to manage symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Crush them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the crushed Silybum marianum powder with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil is warm and the mixture is well combined.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Store the Silybum marianum cream in a cool, dark place. Apply 1-2 teaspoons of the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation caused by chronic hepatitis. Ginger's antioxidant properties also neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells, promoting liver health. Additionally, ginger's ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines helps alleviate liver damage and promote recovery. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Zingiber officinale creams may help manage symptoms and promote liver function in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger oil (Zingiber officinale), 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add ginger oil, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera gel. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container or a tin. Let it cool and solidify completely. This may take about 30 minutes to an hour, depending on the temperature.
Once solidified, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Apply as needed to soothe and calm the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property helps reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with chronic hepatitis. Glycyrrhizin also has antioxidant properties, which protect the liver cells from oxidative stress. Licorice root, the source of glycyrrhizin, contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-viral and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and reduce liver damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting liver cells, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may provide relief from chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of glycerin, and 2 tablespoons of powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Combine the distilled water, glycerin, and powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root in a separate saucepan. Heat until the Glycyrrhiza glabra root dissolves.
Slowly add the water mixture to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, store the Glycyrrhiza glabra cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and damage. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to improve liver function and reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with chronic hepatitis. Bilobalide has been found to have a protective effect on liver cells, while ginkgolides help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the liver. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver health, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis and improve liver function.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba extract, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add Ginkgo biloba extract, vitamin E oil, and lavender oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120-130°F. This is the ideal temperature for applying to skin.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use. Apply a thin layer to affected skin areas as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus creams contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpene lactones. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by chronic hepatitis. Saponins, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the growth of liver cancer cells and reduce liver fibrosis. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones help protect the liver from oxidative stress and promote its natural healing processes. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver health, Cynara scolymus creams may be a beneficial supplement for individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Cynara scolymus herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Aloe Vera gel, 1/4 cup of Coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of Cynara scolymus extract, 1 teaspoon of Vitamin E oil.
Mix Aloe Vera gel and Coconut oil in a bowl until well combined.
Add Cynara scolymus extract and Vitamin E oil to the bowl. Stir until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a sterilized container. Store in the refrigerator.
Apply 1-2 tablespoons of the cream to the affected skin 2-3 times a day, as directed by a healthcare professional for chronic hepatitis treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata creams contains boswellic acids, particularly acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA), 3-acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBBA), and beta-boswellic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis. The boswellic acids also inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which contribute to liver damage. Additionally, boswellic acids have antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can harm liver cells. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Boswellia serrata creams may help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons Boswellia serrata extract, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add Boswellia serrata extract, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small bowl or a silicone mold. Let it cool and solidify at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
Once solidified, remove the cream from the mold or bowl and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use as needed for chronic hepatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains bioactive constituents like astragaloside IV, astragaloside I, and calycosin, which have been studied for their effects on chronic hepatitis. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and damage. Astragalus membranaceus creams also contain flavonoids, which can help protect the liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. The saponins in Astragalus membranaceus creams can improve liver function and promote the regeneration of liver cells. By reducing inflammation and protecting liver cells, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help manage chronic hepatitis symptoms and improve liver health.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for chronic hepatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Astragalus membranaceus tincture (1:5), and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly, then stir in Astragalus membranaceus tincture and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once set, the Astragalus membranaceus cream is ready for use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chronic hepatitis from forming. Some herbs like milk thistle and dandelion root have been studied for their potential to protect the liver from damage. These herbs may help remove toxins and improve liver function, which could reduce the risk of chronic hepatitis. The effects of herbal tea on hepatitis are still being researched.
faq_safe: While herbal teas may be soothing, consuming them daily for chronic hepatitis isn't recommended. Some teas, like milk thistle, may help, but their effectiveness is still being studied. Others might interact with medications or worsen liver problems. It's essential to check the ingredients and potential risks before making them a regular part of your treatment.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help with chronic hepatitis, but it's hard to predict exactly when results will show. Some people notice improvements in a few weeks, like reduced fatigue or fewer symptoms. Others may take a few months to see noticeable changes in their liver function or overall health. It's different for everyone.
faq_time: For chronic hepatitis, it's best to drink herbal tea on an empty stomach in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the body to absorb the tea's benefits without any interference from food.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for chronic hepatitis, possibly causing problems. Some teas, like St. John's Wort, may make medications less effective. Others, like milk thistle, may increase the risk of side effects. If you're taking prescription medications for hepatitis, it's a good idea to talk to your pharmacist about any herbal teas you're using.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and dealing with chronic hepatitis, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas can affect liver function or stimulate the uterus, which may harm the baby. Milk thistle, for example, is sometimes used to support liver health, but its safety in pregnancy is unknown. It's best to avoid herbal teas altogether to ensure the baby's safety.
faq_breastfeeding: It's not clear if herbal teas are safe for chronic hepatitis while breastfeeding. Some herbal teas may interact with medications or worsen liver damage. If you're breastfeeding and have chronic hepatitis, it's best to choose teas that are known to be safe, such as peppermint or chamomile. However, always check the ingredients and talk to someone you trust about any concerns.
title_2: Chronic hepatitis in liver transplant recipients: 8 herbal creams to explore
